channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: As Israel advances in Gaza, many exhausted families flee again. Some can't bear it
item.description: As Israel orders wide new evacuations across the Gaza Strip, Palestinians say they are crushed by exhaustion and hopelessness at the prospect of fleeing once again.
item.dc:creator: Fatma Khaled And Mohammad Jahjouh, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Survivors still being found from Myanmar earthquake, but hopes begin to fade as deaths exceed 2,700
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — Rescue workers saved a 63-year-old woman from the rubble of a building in Myanmar's capital on Tuesday, but hope was fading of finding many more survivors of the violent earthquake that killed more than 2,700 people, compounding a humanitarian crisis caused by a civil war.
item.dc:creator: DAVID RISING, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A wary Europe awaits Rubio with NATO's future on the line
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio is traveling this week to a gathering of top diplomats from NATO countries.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Lee And Lorne Cook, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Paraguay recalls ambassador to Brazil and suspends dam talks over espionage revelations
item.description: Paraguay says it's recalling its ambassador to Brazil a day after Brazilian authorities acknowledged that their country’s intelligence agency had spied on Paraguayan officials in 2022.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Fourth US soldier missing in Lithuania found dead
item.description: “Tragically, three soldiers were found deceased in Lithuania yesterday, and it pains me to confirm that today the fourth soldier was also found deceased,” White House said.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, NewsNation

item.title: Most in survey of Europeans support retaliatory tariffs against US
item.description: Most Western Europeans are supportive of the continent’s intent to fire back when President Trump moves forward with tariffs set to take effect Wednesday, according to a survey released on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: At just 29, Jordan Bardella inherits the French far-right spotlight, whether he’s ready or not
item.description: PARIS (AP) — He wears his suits like armor, smiles like a pop star and boasts more than 2 million followers on TikTok.
item.dc:creator: THOMAS ADAMSON, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Putin conscripts 160K men as Russia eyes Ukraine offensive
item.description: Russia's largest military draft in 14 years sees 160,000 men called up as Putin plans a spring offensive on Ukraine. Despite stalled U.S. ceasefire talks, the Kremlin insists conscripts won't fight.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Reporter's Notebook: Aftershock from a political 'earthquake' as Le Pen barred from presidential run in 2027
item.description: Le Pen called her ban from running a "nuclear bomb" by the system, after a court convicted her of embezzlement and blocked her from office for five years.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Netanyahu accuses Israeli police of trying to 'topple' his government
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu is outraged over the ongoing Qatar probe and says it is a political investigation that is meant to prevent the firing of Shin Bet chief Ronen Bar.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Amsterdam knife attacker who injured 2 Americans suspected of having 'terrorist motive,' prosecutors say
item.description: The Ukrainian suspect who stabbed five people in Amsterdam last week, including two Americans, likely had a "terrorist motive," Dutch prosecutors say.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pope's work during convalescence includes clearing path for saints from Venezuela, Papua New Guinea
item.description: VATICAN CITY (AP) — Pope Francis spends part of his days at his desk working and concelebrates Mass daily in his private chapel, the Vatican said Tuesday, in signs of continued improvements during his convalescence at the Vatican after beating life-threatening double pneumonia during a five-week hos
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN BARRY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A Palestinian from the West Bank is first detainee under 18 to die in Israeli prison, officials say
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — A teenager from the West Bank who was held in an Israeli prison for six months without being charged died after collapsing in unclear circumstances, becoming the first Palestinian under 18 to die in Israeli detention, officials said.
item.dc:creator: JULIA FRANKEL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Musk slams Le Pen ruling, says it will 'backfire' like Trump's as some on global right face legal troubles
item.description: Marine Le Pen’s sentencing in France has drawn global attention, with parallels made to legal battles faced by President Donald Trump, Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu, and former Brazilian President Jair Bolsonaro, among other right-wing politicians.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Germany sees Ukraine truce efforts as deadlocked, while China says the talks are encouraging
item.description: Germany’s chief diplomat is describing U.S. President Donald Trump’s efforts to secure a truce in the three-year war between Russia and Ukraine as deadlocked.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Volcano erupts in southwestern Iceland after a nearby town and spa are evacuated
item.description: A volcano in southwestern Iceland that has erupted repeatedly for more than a year again belched lava and smoke into the air, just hours after authorities evacuated the few remaining residents of a nearby fishing village.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Israeli military orders the evacuation of Gaza's southern city of Rafah
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — Israel's military on Monday issued sweeping evacuation orders covering Rafah and nearby areas, indicating it could soon launch another major ground operation in the Gaza Strip's southernmost city.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and FATMA KHALED, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What to know about the ruling barring Marine Le Pen from seeking public office for five years
item.description: PARIS (AP) — French far-right leader Marine Le Pen has been sentenced to a five-year ban on running for public office for embezzling EU funds, a major political earthquake. The ruling, which takes effect immediately, is likely to bar her from running in France’s next presidential election.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Chinese military conducts large-scale drills around Taiwan
item.description: The Chinese military has conducted large-scale drills around Taiwan as it again warned the self-ruled island against seeking formal independence.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump highlights Friedman, Grenell as possible UN ambassadors to replace Stefanik
item.description: President Trump on Monday said there are “maybe 30” people interested in serving as ambassador to the United Nations after he pulled Rep. Elise Stefanik’s (R-N.Y.) nomination.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: Trump wants new Iran nuclear deal after he withdrew from the first
item.description: Trump withdrew the U.S from the deal during his first term, claiming it was “the worst and most one-sided transaction.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, NewsNation

item.title: Iran's Khamenei warns of 'strong blow' as Trump threatens to drop bombs, Putin silent on US ire
item.description: Iran and Russia took decisively different approaches in response to President Donald Trump's threats as Tehran vowed action against any "mischief" while the Kremlin remains tight-lipped.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Greenland's prime minister says US will not 'get' island
item.description: Jens-Frederik Nielsen, Greenland's prime minister, said in a Facebook post on Sunday that the United States will not be able to annex the resource-rich island in the Atlantic.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Chinese official visits Moscow as Russia and Ukraine discuss Trump's proposal to end war
item.description: BEIJING (AP) — Chinese Foreign Minister Wang Yi traveled to Moscow on Monday for talks with his counterpart, Sergei Lavrov, as Russia and Ukraine discuss U.S. President Donald Trump’s proposal to end the 3-year-old war.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Declining Eid travel and spending in Indonesia and discrimination in India dampen holiday spirit
item.description: JAKARTA, Indonesia (AP) — The usual festive mood of Eid al-Fitr holiday to mark the end of the Islamic holy month of Ramadan has been subdued in Indonesia this year as people grapple with soaring prices for food, clothing and essential goods.
item.dc:creator: NINIEK KARMINI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump finds fault with both Putin and Zelenskyy as he tries to push for deal to end war in Ukraine
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump lashed out at both Vladimir Putin and Volodymyr Zelenskyy on Sunday, expressing frustration with the Russian and Ukrainian leaders as he struggles to forge a truce to end the war.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Chair of African charity Prince Harry co-founded says the royal orchestrated a bullying campaign
item.description: LONDON (AP) — The chairperson of an African charity co-founded by Prince Harry accused the royal on Sunday of orchestrating a bullying and harassment campaign to try to force her out as she pushed back following his abrupt resignation from the organization.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN MELLEY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: The Latest: Countries sending humanitarian aid after Myanmar earthquake
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — The death toll from Myanmar's powerful 7.7 magnitude earthquake keeps climbing amid rescue efforts. The military government said Saturday that 1,644 people have been killed, with thousands of others injured and dozens missing.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Myanmar's earthquake death toll jumps to 1,644 as more bodies are recovered from the rubble
item.description: Myanmar’s ruling military confirmed death toll from a devastating 7.7 magnitude earthquake rose to 1,644, as more bodies were pulled from the rubble of the scores of buildings that collapsed.
item.dc:creator: David Rising, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Countries that have issued travel advisories for US
item.description: Denmark, Finland, France and Germany noted the U.S. requires individuals entering to choose "male" or "female" on ESTA and visa applications.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Signal leak: Trump adviser Mike Waltz may have to go, Warner says
item.description: “There needs to be accountability,” said Sen. Warner, who faults Trump's inner circle with a number of security gaffes.
item.dc:creator: Michael Ramsey, NewsNation

item.title: Missing US soldiers in Lithuania killed: NATO chief
item.description: Four U.S. soldiers who went missing while training in Lithuania have died, NATO Secretary-General Mark Rutte said Wednesday. Speaking to reporters during a trip to Warsaw, Rutte said he had received word of the deaths of the four American troops.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, NewsNation

item.title: Gabbard, Ratcliffe, Patel criticized over Signal chat at hearing
item.description: Though the hearing is about the 2025 Annual Worldwide Threats Assessment, officials were questioned about a Signal group chat leak.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, NewsNation

item.title: JD Vance joining his wife for Greenland trip on Friday
item.description: The delegation is set to tour the northernmost security installation for the U.S. military, the Space Force's Pituffik Space Base.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dempsey, NewsNation
